questions.question: What are the four requirements for successful method overriding in C++?
expectedPoints: The function must be declared with the 'virtual' keyword in the base class
The function must have the same name in both base and derived classes
The function must have the same return type (with covariance exceptions)
The function must have the same parameter list

questions.question: Explain the virtual table mechanism and how it enables runtime polymorphism in C++.
expectedPoints: Virtual tables (vtables) are created by the compiler for classes with virtual functions
Each object contains a hidden vptr pointer to its class's vtable
The vtable contains pointers to the virtual functions of the class
During virtual function calls, the program follows the vptr to the vtable and then to the correct function
This enables late binding where the function implementation is determined at runtime based on the actual object type
